next_steps: Perform BRAF V600E mutation analysis on bone biopsy tissue
Perform immunohistochemistry for CD1a, langerin, and histiocytic markers (CD68, CD163, FXIIIa)
Evaluate for systemic organ involvement including cardiac MRI and echocardiography
Measure serum IgG4 levels and inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR)
Consider whole-body FDG-PET/CT to assess for classic symmetrical long-bone involvement
Consult pathology with expertise in histiocytic disorders for specialized slide review

differential_diagnoses: IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD) with systemic involvement including osteitis
next_steps: Perform BRAF V600E mutation analysis on the tibial biopsy specimen
Order immunohistochemistry for S100 and CD1a to exclude RDD and LCH
Measure serum IgG4, IgG subclass levels, and serum free light chains
Perform cardiac MRI to assess for periaortic or cardiac involvement common in ECD/IgG4-RD
Complete expert dermatopathological/hematopathological review of all tissue biopsy blocks
Arrange ophthalmologic and endocrinologic evaluation to screen for pituitary or lacrimal involvement
